---
title: Using new workflow to find UBC cluster markers
date: 2024-07-08
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

# Goal of this analysis

1. Find markers with new workflow (SCTransform then PrepSCTFindMarkers)

# Files and directories

## Input

CCA RL file is found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs`

## Output

The output can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240708`

# Process and Results

Using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment

Lets load in the required libraries
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(ggrepel)
```

Loading in the Seurat object
```{r eval=FALSE}
set.seed(1)
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240708"
rl_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs")
```

Step 1: Filter RL lineage for human UBC cells
```{r eval=FALSE}
ubc_cells <- rl_cca %>%
  subset(subset = species == "human") %>%
  subset(subset = broad_cell_type == "UBC") 
```

Step 2: Separate the UBC cells by dataset
```{r eval=FALSE}
ald <- subset(ubc_cells, subset = dataset_name == "Aldinger_full_cerebellum_human")
sepp <- subset(ubc_cells, subset = dataset_name == "Sepp_full_cerebellum_human")
```

Step 3: Take row sums for each gene in count matrix, keep genes expressed in 10 or more cells
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Get counts slot from Seurat object
counts_ald <- GetAssayData(object = ald, slot = "counts")
counts_sepp <- GetAssayData(object = sepp, slot = "counts")

# If a count for a gene in a cell is greater than 0, set as TRUE (= 1)
nonzero_ald <- counts_ald > 0
nonzero_sepp <- counts_sepp > 0

# If 10 or more cells are TRUE, keep the gene. Each TRUE value = 1. Taking the sum of all the cells for that gene
keep_ald_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_ald) >= 10
keep_sepp_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_sepp) >= 10

# Get the genes names that we are keeping
ald_genes <- rownames(ald)[keep_ald_genes]
sepp_genes <- rownames(sepp)[keep_sepp_genes]
```

Step 4: Take intersecting genes between the datasets
```{r eval=FALSE}
intersecting_genes <- intersect(ald_genes, sepp_genes)  
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Note that the number of genes in the Aldinger and Sepp datasets are the same as they were merged together before
> print(length(rownames(ald)))
[1] 52672

> print(length(rownames(sepp)))
[1] 52672

> print(length(ald_genes))
[1] 13974

> print(length(sepp_genes))
[1] 16542

> print(length(intersecting_genes))
[1] 10774
```

Step 5: Filter each dataset for intersecting genes
```{r eval=FALSE}
ald <- subset(ald, features = intersecting_genes)
sepp <- subset(sepp, features = intersecting_genes)
```

Step 6: Run SCTransform on each dataset, regressing out (CC.Difference)
```{r eval=FALSE}
ald <- SCTransform(ald, vars.to.regress = "CC.Difference", variable.features.n = 5000)
sepp <- SCTransform(sepp, vars.to.regress = "CC.Difference", variable.features.n = 5000)
```

Step 7: Merge the datasets back together
```{r eval=FALSE}
ubc_merged <- merge(x = ald, y = sepp)
```

Step 8: Run PrepSCTFindMarkers()
```{r eval=FALSE}
ubc_merged <- PrepSCTFindMarkers(object = ubc_merged)
```

Step 9: Run FindMarkers()
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Releveling based on alphabetical then numeric order
levels_index <- str_order(
  unique(ubc_merged$new_clusters),
  numeric = TRUE
)
ubc_merged$new_clusters <- fct_relevel(ubc_merged$new_clusters, levels(ubc_merged$new_clusters)[levels_index])

# Setting the Idents to be the cluster name because FindMarkers() uses the Ident
Idents(ubc_merged) <- "new_clusters"

# Creating empty list to store markers
ubc_marker_list <- list()

# Find markers for each cluster
for (cluster in levels(Idents(ubc_merged))) {
  ubc_markers <- FindMarkers(
    object = ubc_merged,
    ident.1 = cluster,
    logfc.threshold = 0,
    min.pct = 0.01,
    only.pos = FALSE,
    assay = "SCT"
  )
  # Append markers to list
  ubc_marker_list[[cluster]] <- ubc_markers
}

# Creating a dataframe from the list of markers
df_ubc <- ubc_marker_list
for (name in names(df_ubc)) {
  # Adding cluster name as a column
  df_ubc[[name]]$cluster <- name
  # Adding gene name as a column
  df_ubc[[name]]$feature <- rownames(df_ubc[[name]])
}

# Stack into one df
combined_df <- do.call(rbind,df_ubc)

write.csv(combined_df, file = paste0(out_directory, "/ubc_markers.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

I have the markers for each UBC cluster stored in the `ubc_marker_list` variable. Now I can create the necessary plots.

## Volcano Plots
```{r eval=FALSE}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/plotting.R")

for(cluster in names(ubc_marker_list)){
    variable_markers <- ubc_marker_list[[cluster]]

    # Adding genes as a column
    variable_markers$feature <- rownames(variable_markers)

    # Setting log2FC threshold
    log_fc_thresh <- 0.5
    
    # Adding column to determine if gene is up regulated or down regulated
    variable_markers <- mutate(
      variable_markers,
      direction = case_when(
        variable_markers$avg_log2FC >= log_fc_thresh ~ "Upregulated",
        variable_markers$avg_log2FC <= -log_fc_thresh ~ "Downregulated",
        variable_markers$avg_log2FC < log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$avg_log2FC > -log_fc_thresh ~ "Not significant"
        )
      )
    variable_markers$direction <- factor(variable_markers$direction, levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated", "Not significant"))

    # Adding column denotating genes to label
    variable_markers$feature_labelled <- ifelse(abs(variable_markers$avg_log2FC) > log_fc_thresh & variable_markers$p_val_adj < 0.05, variable_markers$feature, NA)

    # Dealing with padjval = 0. -log10(0) = infinity therefore we need to deal with the 0 value by setting it as something small
    non_zero <- variable_markers$p_val_adj[variable_markers$p_val_adj != 0]
    # Find the smallest value
    min_val <- min(non_zero)
    # Multiply the smallest value by 0.1 and assign to p_val_adj values
    variable_markers$p_val_adj <- ifelse(variable_markers$p_val_adj == 0, min_val*exp(-1), variable_markers$p_val_adj)
    
    # Creating the volcano plot
    plt <- make_volcano(
      data = variable_markers,
      log_fc = avg_log2FC,
      log_pval = -log10(p_val_adj),
      direction = direction,
      gene_labels = feature_labelled,
      log_fc_thresh = c(-log_fc_thresh, log_fc_thresh),
      log_pval_thresh = -log10(0.05),
      label_num_genes = TRUE
    ) + 
    theme_classic() +
    labs(x = expression(log[2]~"(fold change)"), y = expression(-log[10]~"(adj. p-value)")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue", "grey")) +
    ggtitle(cluster) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(title = NULL))

    volcano_name <- paste0(cluster, "_volcano_plot.png")
    ggsave(filename = volcano_name, plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
}
```

![Volcano plot for UBC_1 looks consistant with previous results](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240708/UBC_1_volcano_plot.png)

## Heatmaps
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Extract markers of interest
markers <- list()
top_features_list <- list()
for(cluster in ubc_marker_list){
  cluster_markers <- cluster %>%
    dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 0, p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
    arrange(desc(avg_log2FC), desc(pct.1)) %>%
    slice_head(n = 20) %>%
    ungroup() -> top_features

  top_features_list <- c(top_features_list, list(top_features))
  markers <- c(markers, list(rownames(cluster_markers)))
}
markers <- unique(unlist(markers))
```

Lets plot the data slot for the matrix
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Extract the SCT assay data from the data slot and convert to matrix
ubc_data <- GetAssayData(ubc_merged, assay = "SCT", slot = "data")

# Converting to matrix form for ComplexHeatmap
ubc_data_matrix <- ubc_data[markers, ] %>%
    as.matrix()

# Extract the metadata
columns <- c("new_clusters","dataset_name")
ubc_metadata <- ubc_merged@meta.data[, columns]

# Order is based on the age progression of the clusters. UBC_5 and UBC_1 are mostely from earlier time points while UBC_4 and UBC_2 are from later points
custom_order <- c("UBC_5", "UBC_1", "UBC_0", "UBC_3", "UBC_4", "UBC_2")

# Reordering metadata based on custom order
ubc_metadata$new_clusters <- factor(ubc_metadata$new_clusters, levels = custom_order)
ubc_metadata <- arrange(ubc_metadata, new_clusters)

# Refactoring so legend is in alphabetical + numeric order
levels_index <- str_order(
  unique(ubc_metadata$new_clusters),
  numeric = TRUE
)
ubc_metadata$new_clusters <- fct_relevel(ubc_metadata$new_clusters, levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)[levels_index])

ubc_metadata$dataset_name <- factor(ubc_metadata$dataset_name, levels = unique(ubc_metadata$dataset_name))

# Making sure data matrix and metadata are alligned
ubc_data_matrix <- ubc_data_matrix[, rownames(ubc_metadata)]
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
png(file.path(out_directory, "ubc_data_heatmap.png"),width=30,height=25,units="in",res=600)

# Setting colour pallette
num_clusters <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters))
num_datasets <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name))

palette <- brewer.pal(num_clusters + num_datasets, "Dark2")

cluster_palette <- palette[1:num_clusters]
names(cluster_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)

dataset_palette <- palette[(num_clusters + 1):(num_clusters + num_datasets)]
names(dataset_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)

# Column annotation
column_annotation <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Group = ubc_metadata$new_clusters,
  Dataset = ubc_metadata$dataset_name,
  col = list(
    Group = cluster_palette,
    Dataset = dataset_palette
  ),
  show_legend = c(
    "Group" = FALSE,  
    "Dataset" = FALSE  
  ),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE,
  simple_anno_size = unit(0.5, "in")
)

cluster_legend <- Legend(labels = names(cluster_palette), 
    title = "UBC Subclusters", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = cluster_palette))

dataset_legend <- Legend(labels = names(dataset_palette), 
    title = "Datasets", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = dataset_palette))

# Heatmap
heatmap_col <- brewer.pal(8, "YlGnBu")
col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 3.5), heatmap_col)

ubc_heatmap <- Heatmap(ubc_data_matrix,
    col = col_fun,
    top_annotation = column_annotation,
    width = unit(20, "in"), height = unit(20, "in"),
    row_title = "Marker Genes", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_title = "UBC Cells", column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_km = 4,
    column_dend_height = unit(3, "in"),
    column_dend_reorder = FALSE,
    row_dend_reorder = TRUE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    show_row_names = TRUE,  
    show_column_names = FALSE,  
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = "Gene Expression",
        title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
        title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
        grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
        legend_height = unit(6, "in")))

draw(ubc_heatmap, 
    ht_gap = unit(1, "in"),
    heatmap_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_list = list(cluster_legend, dataset_legend))

dev.off()
```

![This colour scale is not great. Perhaps we should go back to the diverging palette](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240708/ubc_data_heatmap.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
png(file.path(out_directory, "sorted_ubc_data_heatmap.png"),width=30,height=25,units="in",res=600)

# Setting colour pallette
num_clusters <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters))
num_datasets <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name))

palette <- brewer.pal(num_clusters + num_datasets, "Dark2")

cluster_palette <- palette[1:num_clusters]
names(cluster_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)

dataset_palette <- palette[(num_clusters + 1):(num_clusters + num_datasets)]
names(dataset_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)

# Column annotation
column_annotation <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Group = ubc_metadata$new_clusters,
  Dataset = ubc_metadata$dataset_name,
  col = list(
    Group = cluster_palette,
    Dataset = dataset_palette
  ),
  show_legend = c(
    "Group" = FALSE,  
    "Dataset" = FALSE  
  ),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE,
  simple_anno_size = unit(0.5, "in")
)

cluster_legend <- Legend(labels = names(cluster_palette), 
    title = "UBC Subclusters", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = cluster_palette))

dataset_legend <- Legend(labels = names(dataset_palette), 
    title = "Datasets", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = dataset_palette))

# Heatmap
heatmap_col <- brewer.pal(8, "YlGnBu")
col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 3.5), heatmap_col)

ubc_heatmap <- Heatmap(ubc_data_matrix,
    col = col_fun,
    top_annotation = column_annotation,
    width = unit(20, "in"), height = unit(20, "in"),
    row_title = "Marker Genes", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_title = "UBC Cells", column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    show_row_names = TRUE,  
    show_column_names = FALSE,  
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = "Gene Expression",
        title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
        title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
        grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
        legend_height = unit(6, "in")))

draw(ubc_heatmap, 
    ht_gap = unit(1, "in"),
    heatmap_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_list = list(cluster_legend, dataset_legend))

dev.off()
```

![You can somewhat see multiple programs, but the colours are not helping](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240708/sorted_ubc_data_heatmap.png)

Lets scale the matrix by normalizing each gene between -1 and 1
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Normalize between -1 and 1
normalize_row <- function(x) {
  return(scale(x))
}

# Normalizes each row (gene)
scaled_ubc_data_matrix <- t(apply(ubc_data_matrix, 1, normalize_row))

# When scale is run on a row, it loses the colnames so we had to add it back
colnames(scaled_ubc_data_matrix) <- colnames(ubc_data_matrix)

# Reordering the matrix based on the metadata dataframe
scaled_ubc_data_matrix <- scaled_ubc_data_matrix[, rownames(ubc_metadata)]
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
png(file.path(out_directory, "scaled_ubc_data_heatmap.png"),width=30,height=25,units="in",res=600)

# Setting colour pallette
num_clusters <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters))
num_datasets <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name))

palette <- brewer.pal(num_clusters + num_datasets, "Dark2")

cluster_palette <- palette[1:num_clusters]
names(cluster_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)

dataset_palette <- palette[(num_clusters + 1):(num_clusters + num_datasets)]
names(dataset_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)

# Column annotation
column_annotation <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Group = ubc_metadata$new_clusters,
  Dataset = ubc_metadata$dataset_name,
  col = list(
    Group = cluster_palette,
    Dataset = dataset_palette
  ),
  show_legend = c(
    "Group" = FALSE,  
    "Dataset" = FALSE  
  ),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE,
  simple_anno_size = unit(0.5, "in")
)

cluster_legend <- Legend(labels = names(cluster_palette), 
    title = "UBC Subclusters", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = cluster_palette))

dataset_legend <- Legend(labels = names(dataset_palette), 
    title = "Datasets", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = dataset_palette))

# Heatmap
heatmap_col <- brewer.pal(5, "RdBu")
col_fun = colorRamp2(c(1, 0.5, 0, -0.5, -1), heatmap_col)

ubc_heatmap <- Heatmap(scaled_ubc_data_matrix,
    col = col_fun,
    top_annotation = column_annotation,
    width = unit(20, "in"), height = unit(20, "in"),
    row_title = "Marker Genes", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_title = "UBC Cells", column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_km = 4,
    column_dend_height = unit(3, "in"),
    column_dend_reorder = FALSE,
    row_dend_reorder = TRUE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    show_row_names = TRUE,  
    show_column_names = FALSE,  
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = "Gene Expression",
        title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
        title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
        grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
        legend_height = unit(6, "in")))

draw(ubc_heatmap, 
    ht_gap = unit(1, "in"),
    heatmap_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_list = list(cluster_legend, dataset_legend))

dev.off()
```

![These colours look a lot better. However, you can better see that some genes have batch effect between datasets](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240708/scaled_ubc_data_heatmap.png)

```{r eval=FALSE}
png(file.path(out_directory, "scaled_sorted_ubc_data_heatmap.png"),width=30,height=25,units="in",res=600)

# Setting colour pallette
num_clusters <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters))
num_datasets <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name))

palette <- brewer.pal(num_clusters + num_datasets, "Dark2")

cluster_palette <- palette[1:num_clusters]
names(cluster_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)

dataset_palette <- palette[(num_clusters + 1):(num_clusters + num_datasets)]
names(dataset_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)

# Column annotation
column_annotation <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Group = ubc_metadata$new_clusters,
  Dataset = ubc_metadata$dataset_name,
  col = list(
    Group = cluster_palette,
    Dataset = dataset_palette
  ),
  show_legend = c(
    "Group" = FALSE,  
    "Dataset" = FALSE  
  ),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE,
  simple_anno_size = unit(0.5, "in")
)

cluster_legend <- Legend(labels = names(cluster_palette), 
    title = "UBC Subclusters", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = cluster_palette))

dataset_legend <- Legend(labels = names(dataset_palette), 
    title = "Datasets", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = dataset_palette))

# Heatmap
heatmap_col <- brewer.pal(5, "RdBu")
col_fun = colorRamp2(c(1, 0.5, 0, -0.5, -1), heatmap_col)

ubc_heatmap <- Heatmap(scaled_ubc_data_matrix,
    col = col_fun,
    top_annotation = column_annotation,
    width = unit(20, "in"), height = unit(20, "in"),
    row_title = "Marker Genes", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_title = "UBC Cells", column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    show_row_names = TRUE,  
    show_column_names = FALSE,  
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = "Gene Expression",
        title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
        title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
        grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
        legend_height = unit(6, "in")))

draw(ubc_heatmap, 
    ht_gap = unit(1, "in"),
    heatmap_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_list = list(cluster_legend, dataset_legend))

dev.off()
```

![You can see the batch effects even clearer here](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240708/scaled_sorted_ubc_data_heatmap.png)

To attempt to reduce the batch effect. Lets scale each gene within each dataset. Hopefully this will produce better visualizations.
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Extract the SCT assay data from the data slot and convert to matrix
ald_data <- GetAssayData(ald, assay = "SCT", slot = "data")

# Converting to matrix form for ComplexHeatmap
ald_data_matrix <- ald_data[markers, ] %>%
    as.matrix()

# Scale data matrix
scaled_ald_data_matrix <- t(apply(ald_data_matrix, 1, normalize_row))
colnames(scaled_ald_data_matrix) <- colnames(ald_data_matrix)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Extract the SCT assay data from the data slot and convert to matrix
sepp_data <- GetAssayData(sepp, assay = "SCT", slot = "data")

# Converting to matrix form for ComplexHeatmap
sepp_data_matrix <- sepp_data[markers, ] %>%
    as.matrix()

# Scale data matrix
scaled_sepp_data_matrix <- t(apply(sepp_data_matrix, 1, normalize_row))
colnames(scaled_sepp_data_matrix) <- colnames(sepp_data_matrix)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Combining the two matricies into one
combined_scaled_ubc_data_matrix <- cbind(scaled_ald_data_matrix, scaled_sepp_data_matrix)
combined_scaled_ubc_data_matrix <- combined_scaled_ubc_data_matrix[, rownames(ubc_metadata)]
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
png(file.path(out_directory, "combined_scaled_sorted_ubc_data_heatmap.png"),width=30,height=25,units="in",res=600)

# Setting colour pallette
num_clusters <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters))
num_datasets <- length(levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name))

palette <- brewer.pal(num_clusters + num_datasets, "Dark2")

cluster_palette <- palette[1:num_clusters]
names(cluster_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$new_clusters)

dataset_palette <- palette[(num_clusters + 1):(num_clusters + num_datasets)]
names(dataset_palette) <- levels(ubc_metadata$dataset_name)

# Column annotation
column_annotation <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Group = ubc_metadata$new_clusters,
  Dataset = ubc_metadata$dataset_name,
  col = list(
    Group = cluster_palette,
    Dataset = dataset_palette
  ),
  show_legend = c(
    "Group" = FALSE,  
    "Dataset" = FALSE  
  ),
  show_annotation_name = FALSE,
  simple_anno_size = unit(0.5, "in")
)

cluster_legend <- Legend(labels = names(cluster_palette), 
    title = "UBC Subclusters", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = cluster_palette))

dataset_legend <- Legend(labels = names(dataset_palette), 
    title = "Datasets", 
    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
    title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
    grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
    grid_height = unit(1, "in"),
    legend_gp = gpar(fill = dataset_palette))

# Heatmap
heatmap_col <- brewer.pal(5, "RdBu")
col_fun = colorRamp2(c(1, 0.5, 0, -0.5, -1), heatmap_col)

ubc_heatmap <- Heatmap(combined_scaled_ubc_data_matrix,
    col = col_fun,
    top_annotation = column_annotation,
    width = unit(20, "in"), height = unit(20, "in"),
    row_title = "Marker Genes", row_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    column_title = "UBC Cells", column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"),
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    row_names_side = "left",
    show_row_names = TRUE,  
    show_column_names = FALSE,  
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = "Gene Expression",
        title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
        title_position = "leftcenter-rot",
        grid_width = unit(0.5, "in"),
        legend_height = unit(6, "in")))

draw(ubc_heatmap, 
    ht_gap = unit(1, "in"),
    heatmap_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_side = "right", 
    annotation_legend_list = list(cluster_legend, dataset_legend))

dev.off()
```

![It got slightly better, with this palette though, it is even more obvious which genes suffer from a batch effect](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240708/combined_scaled_sorted_ubc_data_heatmap.png)
